Okay, so what is the communication cycle? Right? These are the preset meetings and reports that move the business forward and provide you with feedback. Okay? So many would say this is the pulse of your business. So it's like, if you want to know how a human is doing, the first thing that a doctor's going to do is they're going to take a pulse, right? And so having a healthy cadence is like having a healthy pulse. Okay? It's the heartbeat of your business. So what is the communication cycle? This is something that we actually created the communication cycles ourselves. I'm sure I combined it from like five other things I've read about, but either way, we post these and these actually come with every job description we have. Okay? It's our internal communication cycle. So one daily communication, weekly, weekly submissions, which is like reports, monthly communication, and then chain of communication. Okay? So we're going to go through daily, weekly, monthly, but first I want to talk about the chain. Okay? So this is on every position and it is how you teach people and it needs to be written. You can't just say this. You teach people a position and how to solve problems without constantly asking you or their manager. Okay? So here's how we teach our team, right? Say the customer presents, this might be for customer support or trainer, right? Customer presents problem beyond realm of knowledge to resolve. First, you want to search within all the content materials provided to you, such as the frequently asked question, product itself, training to retrieve an answer, right? Two, you then want to reach out to your peers. So if you're a trainer, you're going to reach out to other trainers before you go to your manager, right? Three, if it is still unresolved and you have not found it yourself and none of your peers have the answer, then you want to go to your manager, right? You will let your manager reach out to the person above them and receive and compile a solution, okay? And so you never skip levels. So if I'm a trainer, I'm not going to reach out to the owner if we have an operations manager. I'm going to reach out to the operations manager, okay? And then the operations manager can determine if they need to reach out to the owner, okay? Five, we're going to present the resolution to the client. So say it is somebody who needed to cancel early. And I'm saying, okay, I'm not sure. It's not written anywhere. I asked my peers, they don't know. I'm going to ask my manager, my manager goes to the owner. The owner gives them the answer. And then I'm going to present that to the client, okay? And then afterwards, I'm going to follow up with the client in a timely manner, say one to two days later to make sure that the issue has remained resolved. So you still, I just want to make sure like everything went through and you saw that your membership is canceled, you know, we really love the time you spent with us and we hope to see you again in the near future, you know, best of luck moving forward, right? So that just comes from customer service. So this is what an internal communication cycle is.
